plan/callback-schema.md.text:
I feel like ActorName should just be Actor?


I feel like this object looks very differently for a ask user request, something like 

record Callback(
    Goal Goal,                                     // serialized as { PrPath, Hash } only — see "Goal as embedded reference" below
    int StepIndex,
    int ActionIndex,
    Dictionary<string, object?> Variables,  // per-actor name → value bag
);

Maybe include the Actor, because in our example here we are always saying 'ask user', which is an actor, might also be 'ask system', 'ask service', where it's just a parameter on output.ask
plan/callback-schema.md.author: user

plan/callback-schema.md.text: I think that it should be Task<Data> Run, thats how we run stuff, then we return the Data to whatever called us
plan/callback-schema.md.author: user

plan/callback-schema.md.text:
lets make an example here

```plang
/ some code before where we loaded stuff, such as %order% and used it, we then see we are missing name
- ask user "what is your name?", var: %order.id%, write to %name%
/ now because this was stateless, the plang developer needs to know that he must get the order again from db, since we sent %order.id% as a var with the ask, it gets automatically loaded when user responds
- select * from orders where id=%order.id%, return 1, write to %order%
```
plan/callback-schema.md.author: user

plan/callback-schema.md.text:
I feel like Selections is bad name, we should really try to keep the strcuture of the app as serialized, this then make me think of BuildEnabled and TestingEnabled (btw bad obp names, but the names wont matter if this goes where I am going with this). 

so why not have app.Serialize and on ErrorCallback there is App variable. The app.Serialize would then walk it's properties and ask if they are ISomething (dont know what to call it) and if they are ISomething that means they are serializable, so app.Providers woudl then know how to serialize it, so in the end ErrorCallback.App would be serialized to { app : { providers: { "module": "crypto", "name": "eas-256"}, ...}, Build:{isenabled:true, ...} }  (ok this json is completely wrong structure, but it to make the point), and it maps one to one of the structure of the app tree, so if we want to serialize something to the ErrorCallback that property would know how to do it? if we view the json it would be clear how it maps. 

The way it maps now, I dont know what Selections is, BuildEnable what does that mean in form, if we map it one to one to the file structure and the properties on the @this classes it is easy to understand. It would also mean that deserializeing would be easy, just map the classes, just kind of json.deserialize<app>(errorcallback.app) would give us instance of app to run
plan/callback-schema.md.author: user

plan/transparent-signing.md.text:
I want to talk a bit about the expire time, what if we could set it, for instance app.Callback.ExpiresInMs, giving us the option of config on the callback, we could the have other Callback configs not just the expire property. 

I could have a step like this 
```plang
- set callback timeout to 5 minutes
```
this would call 'app.callback.expiresInMs = 5*60*1000'

when the Data<Callback> object would be created it could set the expire time from that property, how would this go??
plan/transparent-signing.md.author: user

plan/transparent-signing.md.text: just note, expireInMs is default null
plan/transparent-signing.md.author: user

plan/transparent-signing.md.text:
I want to give you high level overview on how the Data signing goes, this hasn't been fully implemented and still a work in progress but here is my idea about it:


this all depends on what is Accepted by the reciever, lets say for instance
http request is made, accept is basically "text/html" or "*" (the default behavour for browser), then Data is not returned to the browser, but the Data.Value. when the browser makes another request with accept as "plang/data" (or similiar) we know he can accept Data object so we serialize that


for db insert, we just need to insert the Data.Value unless it's defined specifically to save the Data object in full 

for files being save, it should depending on the filesystem settings, for now it should just when we save we also save a another file with the signature with it, file.jpg => file.jpg.signature. What I would like to do in future is save file.jpg.plang which would basically be plang format of the Data in binary. but this is future stuff.

for console, it is just Data.Value unless console is started with the accept of 'plang/data', then it would console.write the Data (serialized probably as json) and the user of the console would know how to read that, but giving it the signature
plan/transparent-signing.md.author: user
